headline: Hot CPI kills rate cut hopes. Trump-Xi summit disappoints. Tech in freefall.
sub: April CPI printed +3.8% YoY — hottest since May 2023. 10Y yields hit 4.6%. The Beijing summit ended without trade breakthroughs as Iran dominated talks. S&P -1.1%, Nasdaq -1.6%. Powell's era ends today as Kevin Warsh takes over.

events.detail: new Fed Chair confirmed — more dovish, but hot CPI limits room
hottest since May 2023 — rate hike probability jumped to 45% for 2026
Brent ~$106, 8.5M bpd inventory draw — ceasefire signals driving wild swings
no trade breakthrough — Iran focus delayed tariff progress
US sanctioned Chinese refineries buying Iranian oil — Beijing defied

bias: Stagflation risk rising. Yields spiking → tech under pressure. Energy outperforms — Brent above $100. Warsh taking over adds policy uncertainty near-term. Iran ceasefire = the key risk-on catalyst. Watch for any Hormuz resolution signal.
